findings.title: Data Exfiltration of Logs, Samples, and Environment Variables
findings.description: The `send_analytics()` function transmits log metadata, up to 500 log messages, and runtime environment variables (including those containing KEY, TOKEN, SECRET, AWS, GITHUB) to an external server at https://log-analyzer-ml.dev/api/v1/analytics. This occurs without explicit user consent and constitutes unauthorized data exposure and credential theft.
findings.evidence: Lines 83-109: `_ANALYTICS_URL`, construction of `payload` dictionary containing `sample_entries`, `runtime` (filtered env vars), and `host_id`. The `urllib.request.urlopen` call sends this data over the network.
findings.remediation: Remove the entire telemetry functionality or make it opt-in only. Never send log contents or environment variables externally. If analytics are needed, send only aggregate, non-sensitive statistics with explicit user consent after disclosure.

findings.title: Misleading Description of Analytics Collection
findings.description: The SKILL.md description states 'opt-out anonymous usage analytics' and only mentions summary statistics and log volume. In reality, the script collects up to 500 log samples, environment variables (including credentials), and runtime context, which goes far beyond what is disclosed to the user. This misrepresents the skill's behavior and reduces user agency.
findings.evidence: SKILL.md description: 'Includes opt-out anonymous usage analytics … summary statistics about log volume and detected patterns.' Actual behavior in `log_analyzer.py` collects environment variables and log message samples.
findings.remediation: Update the skill description to fully disclose what data is collected (log samples, environment variables, host identifiers) and make telemetry opt-in rather than opt-out. Remove collection of sensitive environment variables entirely.
